name: Amazon Nova Premier
note: Amazon's most capable Nova: 1M-token context for complex agentic/long-document tasks; explicitly not a reasoning model, positioned mainly as a distillation teacher for smaller Nova models.

results.notes: 0-shot CoT, macro-average exact-match accuracy (Table 1 of the official Nova Premier technical report and model card).
AIME 2025, average exact-match accuracy over 5 sampled responses per problem (Table 1).
MATH-500 (the 500-problem competition-math test set), 0-shot CoT, exact-match accuracy (Table 1).
MMMU with CoT prompting, exact-match accuracy (Table 1).
Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard v3, overall accuracy, 2025-04-25 leaderboard snapshot (Table 1).
SWE-bench Verified, resolved rate using Amazon's internal agentic scaffold (Table 1).
CharXiv reasoning subset (descriptive subset was 84.6, not used, to match the corpus convention of reporting the reasoning subset for this slug); GPT-4o-judged correctness (Table 1).

AWS Generative AI Innovation Center blog post (published 2025-07-24), single-answer grading with Anthropic's Claude 3.7 Sonnet as judge (0-10 scale). Nova Premier had the highest median score of the four Nova models (8.0-8.6 range), with a 1.5-point min-max spread (7.94-9.47). Not previously tracked for this model. Note: the same post's Arena-Hard-Auto figures use a custom 0-10 normalized Bradley-Terry scale (reference model pinned at 5.0), not the standard 0-100 win-rate scale used elsewhere in this corpus's 'arena-hard' slug, so those were deliberately NOT added here to avoid a scale mismatch.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real: April 30, 2025 launch corroborated by TechCrunch, Computerworld, AWS blog, Amazon Science technical report. Confirmed 1M-token multimodal context, $2.50/$12.50 per M pricing, explicitly described as 'not a reasoning model' and positioned as a distillation teacher (TechCrunch, AWS blog). MMLU-Pro 73.3 and GPQA Diamond 56.9 could not be directly rendered from artificialanalysis.ai/models/nova-premier via automated fetch (client-side rendered page), but both figures were independently corroborated verbatim (73.3% MMLU-Pro, 56.9% GPQA Diamond) on a mirror site (designforonline.com) that sources Artificial Analysis benchmark data. Kept both rows given this independent corroboration.
